executive_brief:
This week marks a definitive shift in the cyber threat landscape: the convergence of state-sponsored espionage, supply chain weaponization, and AI-accelerated exploitation has collapsed the window of defense. North Korea's UNC1069 executed a $285 million theft from Drift Protocol while simultaneously compromising the Axios NPM package, proving that social engineering and supply chain attacks are now parallel tracks for the same adversary. Simultaneously, China-linked Storm-1175 has weaponized a '24-hour kill chain,' deploying Medusa ransomware within a day of initial access by chaining zero-days and N-days. The speed of compromise now outpaces traditional patch cycles. 

The second critical vector is the weaponization of trust in AI and open-source infrastructure. Attackers are abusing GitHub as Command-and-Control infrastructure, exploiting AI agent vulnerabilities in Flowise and Grafana for zero-click data exfiltration, and leveraging the recent Claude Code leak to distribute infostealers. This is not background noise; it is a systemic erosion of the software supply chain. With CISA issuing emergency directives for Fortinet and TrueConf flaws, and unpatched Windows zero-days like BlueHammer circulating in the wild, the margin for error has vanished. Security leaders must treat every open-source dependency and AI integration as a potential breach vector.

threat_stories.headline: The Drift Heist: UNC1069's Six-Month Social Engineering Campaign Yields $285M
threat_stories.narrative:
North Korea-linked threat actor UNC1069 executed a sophisticated financial heist against the Drift Protocol, draining $285 million in cryptocurrency. This was not a technical exploit but a six-month social engineering operation where attackers impersonated a trading firm to gain trust and eventually seize Security Council powers [38]. The theft occurred in seconds, highlighting the fragility of multi-signature governance when human elements are compromised. 

UNC1069 did not stop at financial theft. The same group orchestrated a supply chain attack against the Axios NPM package by hijacking the maintainer's account [1]. This dual-pronged approach—financial theft via social engineering and infrastructure compromise via supply chain—demonstrates a strategic shift toward long-term, high-value targeting. The Axios compromise injected malicious code into a widely used library, threatening downstream applications globally. 

The operation utilized fake LinkedIn and Slack profiles to target Node.js maintainers, bypassing traditional perimeter defenses by attacking the human layer of the software supply chain [69]. This campaign confirms that UNC1069 is capable of sustained, multi-vector operations that blend financial gain with strategic infrastructure disruption. The speed of the Drift theft contrasts sharply with the months-long preparation, proving that patience is a weapon.

timeline.event: UNC1069 begins social engineering campaign targeting Drift Protocol personnel
Attackers seize Security Council powers and drain $285M from Drift
Google attributes Axios npm supply chain attack to UNC1069
timeline.significance: Confirms dual-track strategy of financial and infrastructure attacks

timeline.event: Discovery of fake LinkedIn/Slack profiles used to target Node.js maintainers

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. Financial loss of $285M confirmed. Axios compromise affects thousands of downstream applications. Governance models for DeFi are proven vulnerable to social engineering.
threat_stories.action_required: Audit all DeFi governance keys and multi-sig signers. Review npm dependencies for Axios and verify maintainer accounts. Implement strict identity verification for all administrative access to blockchain protocols.

threat_stories.headline: Storm-1175's 24-Hour Kill Chain: Medusa Ransomware Weaponizes Zero-Days
threat_stories.narrative:
China-linked threat actor Storm-1175 has operationalized a '24-hour kill chain,' moving from initial access to ransomware deployment in less than a day [157]. The group is actively exploiting a combination of zero-day and N-day vulnerabilities to breach networks and deploy Medusa ransomware. This velocity renders traditional patch cycles obsolete; by the time a patch is available, the network is already encrypted. 

Storm-1175 has exploited over 16 vulnerabilities across enterprise products since 2023, chaining them to enable persistence, credential theft, and security tool tampering [218]. The group's ability to weaponize zero-days within a week of disclosure indicates access to advanced exploit development capabilities. This is not opportunistic scanning; it is targeted, high-velocity warfare against internet-facing systems. 

The Medusa ransomware variant is designed to exfiltrate data and encrypt systems rapidly, minimizing the window for defenders to respond. The group's focus on internet-facing systems suggests a preference for high-impact targets where downtime is most costly. This campaign represents a significant escalation in ransomware tactics, moving from opportunistic infection to surgical strikes.

timeline.event: Microsoft links Medusa ransomware affiliate to zero-day attacks
timeline.significance: Confirms zero-day usage in ransomware campaigns

timeline.event: Storm-1175 exploits zero-days to deploy Medusa within 24 hours
Discovery of 16+ vulnerabilities chained by Storm-1175

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. Ransomware deployment within 24 hours of breach. High risk to internet-facing enterprise systems. Traditional patch management is insufficient.
threat_stories.action_required: Implement network segmentation to isolate internet-facing systems. Deploy EDR solutions with behavioral analysis. Prioritize patching of internet-facing assets immediately upon disclosure.

threat_stories.headline: The AI Agent Vulnerability Wave: Flowise, Grafana, and Claude Code
threat_stories.narrative:
A new class of vulnerabilities is emerging in AI-powered platforms, allowing attackers to execute code or exfiltrate data without user interaction. Flowise, an open-source AI agent builder, is under active exploitation for a maximum-severity RCE vulnerability (CVE-2025-59528) [172]. Attackers are injecting malicious JavaScript into the CustomMCP node, gaining full system control. 

Simultaneously, Grafana's AI components are vulnerable to 'GrafanaGhost,' a chained exploit using indirect prompt injection and protocol-relative URL bypasses to exfiltrate enterprise data [193]. This zero-click attack tricks the AI into generating requests that embed sensitive data into outbound image rendering requests. 

The recent leak of Claude Code source code has been weaponized by threat actors to distribute Vidar infostealer via fake GitHub repositories [113]. This demonstrates that leaked AI code is not just a privacy issue but a direct vector for malware distribution. The convergence of AI agent vulnerabilities and supply chain attacks creates a 'trusting trust problem' where the security tool itself is the compromise.

timeline.significance: Provides attackers with code to weaponize
Active exploitation of AI agent RCE

threat_stories.impact_assessment: High. Active exploitation of AI platforms. Risk of data exfiltration and system compromise via AI agents. Leaked AI code used for malware distribution.
threat_stories.action_required: Update Flowise to version 3.0.6 or later. Disable AI/LLM features in Grafana if not required. Audit GitHub repositories for unauthorized Claude Code clones.

threat_stories.headline: Critical Infrastructure Siege: Iran Targets US PLCs
threat_stories.narrative:
Iranian-affiliated APT actors are actively targeting internet-facing Operational Technology (OT) devices in the United States, specifically Rockwell Automation/Allen-Bradley PLCs [230]. The actors use configuration software to establish connections to publicly exposed PLCs without sufficient hardening, leading to project file extraction and manipulation of HMI and SCADA display data. 

This campaign targets critical infrastructure sectors including water and energy facilities [244]. The attackers deploy Dropbear SSH for remote access, maintaining persistence within the OT environment. The use of CVE-2021-22681 (Authentication Bypass Vulnerability Found in Logix Controllers) indicates a focus on legacy vulnerabilities that are often unpatched in OT environments. 

The FBI and Pentagon have issued warnings regarding this activity, highlighting the risk of disruption to US critical infrastructure [224]. This is not espionage; it is preparation for disruption. The targeting of PLCs suggests a capability to physically impact industrial processes, posing a national security threat.

timeline.event: CISA Advisory AA26-097A issued regarding Iranian PLC attacks
timeline.significance: Official government warning of active targeting

timeline.event: FBI and Pentagon warn of Iran targeting OT
timeline.significance: Elevates threat level to national security

timeline.event: Discovery of internet-exposed PLCs being manipulated

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. Risk of physical disruption to water and energy facilities. Legacy OT vulnerabilities are being actively exploited.
threat_stories.action_required: Disconnect PLCs from the public-facing internet. Implement secure gateways. Enable multifactor authentication for external access. Keep devices updated with manufacturer patches.

threat_stories.headline: The Zero-Day Emergency: Fortinet, Chrome, and Windows
threat_stories.narrative:
This week saw a surge in actively exploited zero-day vulnerabilities across critical infrastructure. Fortinet released an emergency hotfix for CVE-2026-35616 in FortiClient EMS, which allows unauthenticated remote code execution via API [131]. CISA added this flaw to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, mandating federal agencies to patch by Friday [154]. 

Google patched a fourth Chrome zero-day of 2026 (CVE-2026-5281) in the Dawn WebGPU implementation, which allows arbitrary code execution [80]. This vulnerability is under active exploitation in the wild. 

Additionally, an unpatched Windows privilege escalation flaw, 'BlueHammer,' was leaked by a disgruntled researcher [162]. This local privilege escalation vulnerability allows attackers to gain SYSTEM rights. The release of exploit code significantly increases the risk of compromise for affected systems. The convergence of these zero-days indicates a period of intense vulnerability weaponization.

timeline.event: Fortinet releases emergency patch for CVE-2026-35616
CISA orders feds to patch Fortinet flaw

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. Active exploitation of Fortinet and Chrome. Unpatched Windows vulnerability poses immediate risk.
threat_stories.action_required: Apply Fortinet emergency hotfix immediately. Update Chrome to latest version. Monitor for unauthorized SYSTEM-level processes on Windows.

actor_profiles.name: UNC1069
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Executed $285M theft from Drift Protocol via social engineering. Compromised Axios NPM package. Targeted Node.js maintainers via fake profiles.

actor_profiles.name: Storm-1175
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Weaponized zero-days to deploy Medusa ransomware within 24 hours. Chained 16+ vulnerabilities for rapid compromise.

actor_profiles.name: TeamPCP
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Exploited Trivy supply chain attack to breach European Commission. Targeted CI/CD pipelines to steal cloud credentials.

actor_profiles.name: APT28
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Exploited SOHO routers (MikroTik, TP-Link) to hijack DNS settings. Targeted Microsoft 365 logins via router DNS hijacking.

actor_profiles.name: Iran-Nexus Threat Actor
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Targeting US critical infrastructure via internet-exposed PLCs. Password spraying on Israeli Microsoft 365 organizations.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Improper access control in FortiClient EMS API allows unauthenticated attackers to bypass authentication and execute arbitrary code.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply emergency hotfix for EMS 7.4.5 and 7.4.6. Upgrade to version 7.4.7.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Use-after-free vulnerability in Dawn WebGPU implementation allowing arbitrary code execution.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Chrome to version 146.0.7680.178 or newer.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Code injection vulnerability in CustomMCP node allowing Remote Code Execution via user-supplied JavaScript.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Flowise to version 3.0.6 or later.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Authentication bypass in Cisco Integrated Management Controller allowing unauthenticated remote admin access.
Flaw allows download and installation of unverified updates, leading to arbitrary code execution.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Address by CISA due date of April 16, 2026. Verify update sources.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: React2Shell vulnerability allowing large-scale credential harvesting and secret theft.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Next.js to the latest patched version.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Missing critical security checks in tabpanel sidebar allowing Remote Code Execution upon file open.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Vim to version 9.2.0272 or later.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Authentication Bypass Vulnerability in Logix Controllers allowing unauthorized access.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Disconnect PLCs from public internet. Enable multifactor authentication.

hunting_leads.title: UNC1069 LNK and GitHub C2 Activity
hunting_leads.context: UNC1069 is using weaponized LNK files to execute PowerShell scripts that connect to GitHub repositories for C2.
hunting_leads.query: ProcessName='cmd.exe' | where ParentProcessName='explorer.exe' | where CommandLine contains '.lnk' | join ProcessName='powershell.exe' on ParentProcessId
indicators: Hangul document metadata in LNK files
Outbound traffic to GitHub API endpoints
Scheduled task creation by unknown scripts

hunting_leads.title: Fortinet EMS API Abuse
hunting_leads.context: CVE-2026-35616 allows unauthenticated API access. Monitor for suspicious API requests to FortiClient EMS.
hunting_leads.query: CommonName='FortiClient EMS' | where EventID contains 'API' | where AuthenticationStatus='Success' | where SourceIP !in (TrustedIPs)

hunting_leads.title: AI Agent Anomalies
hunting_leads.context: Flowise and Grafana AI agents are being exploited for RCE and data exfiltration.
hunting_leads.query: ProcessName='node' | where CommandLine contains 'CustomMCP' | where CommandLine contains 'Function()', ProcessName='grafana' | where CommandLine contains 'img-src'
indicators: Outbound image rendering requests from Grafana
Unusual JavaScript execution in AI agents

hunting_leads.title: Windows BlueHammer Privilege Escalation
hunting_leads.context: Unpatched Windows LPE vulnerability allows SYSTEM access. Monitor for unauthorized SYSTEM-level processes.

hunting_leads.title: PLC Configuration Access
hunting_leads.context: Iranian actors are targeting internet-exposed PLCs. Monitor for unauthorized configuration access.
indicators: Connections to PLCs from public IPs

{'summary': 'Malicious .cmd script campaign leveraging PowerShell for privilege escalation, AV bypass, and persistence via scheduled tasks.', 'severity': 'high', 'affected_products': ['Microsoft Windows', 'Windows Defender', 'PowerShell'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': '', 'workarounds': ['Disable script execution via Group Policy', 'Monitor for unauthorized Add-MpPreference commands', 'Block access to is.gd and search.app domains'], 'references': ['Securityaffairs: Image or Malware? Read until the end and answer in comments :)'], 'integrity_impact': 'high', 'description': "The article details a malware campaign delivered via phishing email containing a malicious .cmd file. The script performs privilege escalation using PowerShell 'RunAs', adds exclusions to Windows Defender to bypass antivirus, downloads a disguised payload (initially .jpg, actually .zip) from a shortened URL, extracts an executable, creates a hidden scheduled task for persistence, and self-deletes after rebooting the system. The specific underlying vulnerability exploited by the final payload is not disclosed, but the delivery mechanism relies on social engineering and script execution.", 'attack_vector': 'network', 'privileges_required': 'none', 'exploit_maturity': 'functional', 'availability_impact': 'high', 'remediation': "Remove Windows Defender exclusions added by the malware, delete the malicious scheduled task ('\\Microsoft\\Windows\\IntelGraphicsTask'), remove the payload files from %LOCALAPPDATA%, and block the identified malicious URLs. Update antivirus definitions and ensure PowerShell execution policies are restricted.", 'exploit_available': 'yes', 'cve_id': '', 'cvss_vector': '', 'user_interaction': 'required', 'scope': 'changed', 'confidentiality_impact': 'high', 'published_date': '2026-04-05', 'discovery_date': ''}

statistics.key_changes: Shift from opportunistic ransomware to high-velocity, zero-day-enabled campaigns (Storm-1175). Emergence of AI agent vulnerabilities as a primary attack vector. State actors targeting OT infrastructure directly.
emerging_trends: Supply chain attacks via AI code leaks (Claude Code)
Router DNS hijacking for credential theft
declining_threats: Traditional phishing (replaced by QR code and social engineering)
Opportunistic ransomware (replaced by targeted zero-day campaigns)
